what: Use non-persistent a16w8 for kv=1024 shapes except the tiny bs<=4 case, while keeping bf16 persistent for kv>=4096
why: The recovered aiter baseline is likely metadata-bound on 1k KV shapes. v134 shows the raw fp8 non-persistent path exists; isolating it to 1k KV should remove persistent setup overhead without disturbing the known-good 8k bf16 persistent path.

what: Use bf16_persist page_size=128 only for bs>=64,kv>=4096 while keeping page_size=64 for bs<=32 8k shapes
why: The successful line of work is larger bf16 persistent pages on 8k. Prior ps=128 failures were on bs=4 and bs=32; those do not rule out bs=64 and bs=256, where metadata/page-count reduction matters more and tolerance may still hold.
override: Earlier ps=128 attempts included bs<=32 shapes that failed tolerance; this variant isolates the larger page size to bs>=64 only.

what: Use non-persistent a16w8 only for bs=32/64 kv=1024, while keeping bs=256,kv=1024 on bf16 persistent and keeping the large-batch 8k ps=128 experiment
why: The 1k metadata overhead argument is strongest on the medium-batch shapes, while earlier history around bs=256,kv<=1024 was mixed. This isolates the non-persistent fp8 route to bs=32/64 and leaves the most accuracy-sensitive large 1k shape on the bf16 persistent path.
override: This is a narrower shape split than v146: it explicitly excludes bs=256,kv=1024 from the non-persistent fp8 route.

what: Restore the valid persistent-only hybrid baseline and test bf16_persist page_size=128 only for bs>=64,kv>=4096
why: The recent failures were caused by unsupported non-persistent bf16+fp8 dispatch, not by the large-page idea itself. This isolates the untested ps=128 hypothesis on the last known-valid routing scheme.
override: This retries the ps=128 concept only after removing the unsupported non-persistent fp8 path that invalidated v147/v148.

what: Use bf16_persist page_size=128 only for bs=256,kv>=4096 on the persistent-only hybrid baseline
why: If ps=128 is too aggressive for bs=64, the bs=256 8k case should still benefit the most from halving page metadata, and it avoids touching the smaller 8k shapes that have shown tighter tolerance behavior.
override: This is narrower than v149: only the single heaviest 8k batch uses page_size=128.

what: Use bf16_persist page_size=128 only for bs=256,kv=1024 while leaving all 8k shapes on their prior persistent settings
why: bs=256,kv=1024 already uses the bf16 persistent path in the corrected hybrid baseline, so increasing page size there is a pure metadata/page-count reduction on the worst 1k shape without perturbing the proven 8k routing.
override: This targets the 1k large-batch case only; it is distinct from the 8k-only page-size experiments.

what: Use a16w8 persistent page_size=16 only for bs=32,kv=1024 on the persistent-only hybrid baseline
why: The current hybrid already uses a16w8 persistent on bs=32,kv=1024. Prior ps=16 evidence was contaminated by the bs=64 shape failing tolerance; isolating bs=32 keeps the potentially lower metadata overhead without taking the known-bad bs=64 route.
override: Earlier ps=16 work covered bs=32/64 together; this variant isolates bs=32 only.

what: Use bf16_persist page_size=128 only for bs=64,kv>=4096 on the persistent-only hybrid baseline
why: v149 beat v150, so the improvement likely did not come from the bs=256-only large-page change. Isolating bs=64 tests whether the medium-large 8k batch is the real beneficiary while keeping bs=256 and the smaller 8k shapes on the proven settings.
override: This isolates the v149 improvement candidate after v150 showed the bs=256-only variant was worse.

what: Add the ranked-benchmark parser banner to the v149 kernel to avoid leaderboard server parse failures
why: v149 passed test and benchmark at 20.61µs, but leaderboard failed with a server-side NoneType parse error before producing a score. A previous archived submission used a printed Ranked Benchmark preamble specifically to stabilize the submit parser.
override: This does not change the kernel math; it only adds the known parser-side workaround on top of the best current candidate.
